C4 projective blocks of width >= 2 expose weight-3 zero-message relation combinations; this must be addressed before security claims.
interpretation: C6 is a diagnostic screening layer, not a certified cryptanalysis result. It corrects the C5 relation-weight heuristic by counting weight-3 projective-line relations in C4. A security-grade design should avoid full projective block dictionaries of width >= 2 or prove that the resulting relation-derived samples do not help known LPN attacks.

C4 projective blocks of width >= 2 expose weight-3 zero-message relation combinations; this must be addressed before security claims.
interpretation: C6 is a diagnostic screening layer, not a certified cryptanalysis result. It corrects the C5 relation-weight heuristic by counting weight-3 projective-line relations in C4. A security-grade design should avoid full projective block dictionaries of width >= 2 or prove that the resulting relation-derived samples do not help known LPN attacks.
